{{Infobox book
| name = Uncrowned
| slug = cradle-07-uncrowned
| precedes = [[Wintersteel (novel)|Wintersteel]]
| follows = [[Underlord (novel)|Underlord]]
| cover = [[File:Cover-cradle-07-uncrowned.jpg|250px]]
| released = 2019
| isbn = 9781959001041
| publisher = Hidden Gnome Publishing
| pages = 355
}}

== Plot summary ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The story begins with [[Kelsa]], who is hiding her exiled father, [[Wei Shi Jaran]], in a secret camp. The sacred turtle [[Orthos]] reveals that [[Kelsa]]'s supposedly dead brother, [[Lindon]], is actually alive and exceptionally powerful, prompting [[Kelsa]] to demand the truth about his survival. Meanwhile, in a different setting, [[Makiel]] The Hound{{!}}the Hound tracks the thief [[Iri]], while [[Lindon]] undergoes rigorous testing by [[Akura Justice]] to determine his eligibility for the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament. During this time, [[Yerin]] pushes her combat limits against [[Eithan]], seeking to master his level of skill.

As a protected guest of the [[Akura clan]], [[Lindon]] faces challenges from [[Pride]], who questions his worthiness. After a skirmish involving the [[Herald]] [[Fury]], [[Lindon]] is settled into a private residence where he meets the [[Sage of the Silver Heart]], [[Charity]]. [[Charity]] explains that the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament is essential for the [[Blackflame Empire]]'s survival against [[dragon]] encroachment, prompting [[Lindon]] to begin an intensive eight-month training regimen. His progress catches the attention of [[Herald]] [[Akura Fury]], who stays to teach [[Lindon]] advanced palm strike techniques. Despite facing several defeats against [[Akura Underlords]] and [[Pride]], [[Lindon]] eventually proves himself by defeating multiple candidates in an Akura clan{{!}}Akura family selection tournament.

The journey to the tournament begins with [[Lindon]] departing on a high-speed cloudship, while [[Yerin]], [[Eithan]], and [[Naru Saeya]] are ambushed by the gold [[dragon]] Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara, leaving them stranded. Upon arriving at the [[Ninecloud Court]], [[Lindon]] encounters the [[Winter Sage]] and witnesses the arrival of the [[dragon]] [[Herald]] [[Xorrus]], who mocks the [[Akura]] ship. After being rescued by [[Min Shuei]], [[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]] arrive just in time for the tournament's opening ceremony, which is abruptly interrupted by the legendary [[Monarch]] [[Northstrider]], who seizes control as the [[judge]].

As the tournament progresses through various trials involving [[hunger madra]], puzzle boxes, and combat, [[Lindon]] uses his companion [[Dross]] to climb the rankings to tenth place. During a tropical island round, [[Lindon]]'s team ambushes Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara and her [[dragon]] allies; however, the battle turns tragic when Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara kills [[Naru Saeya]]. In a desperate move, [[Lindon]] uses a crown to neutralize the [[dragon]], though [[Eithan]] is seemingly dissolved by her fire. Following this, [[Lindon]] faces [[Naian Blackflame]] in an arena battle; while he successfully stabilizes [[Naian]]'s spirit, Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara executes the prisoner. This loss is punctuated by [[Northstrider]] delivering a chilling warning: "[[The dragon advances]]."

The tournament enters its final stages with high-stakes matches, including [[Yerin]]'s blindfolded victory over [[Altavian Arelius]]. As the brackets are reshuffled by [[Northstrider]], [[Lindon]] is unexpectedly forced into a single-elimination duel against his close friend, [[Yerin]]. After an intense clash of [[Archlord madra]] and [[Blackflame]] that leaves the arena in ruins, [[Lindon]] loses the match. The tournament takes a cosmic turn when a heavenly messenger named [[Kiuran]] arrives to announce a new grand prize—the divine weapon [[Penance]]—and reveals that the [[Abidan]] are recruiting to combat [[the Mad King]]. As [[Lindon]] attempts to warn [[Yerin]] of the danger this prize poses, he is intercepted by [[Northstrider]].}}

== Chapter-by-chapter ==
* '''Chapter 1:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Kelsa]] leads the sacred turtle [[Orthos]] to a hidden camp where she has been hiding her blind, exiled father, [[Wei Shi Jaran]]. After [[Orthos]] reveals he was once a powerful [[Truegold]] and hints that [[Kelsa]]'s supposedly dead brother, [[Lindon]], is actually alive and exceptionally strong, [[Kelsa]] shares the traumatic history of how their clan was persecuted by the Heaven’s Glory School{{!}}Heaven's Glory School. The chapter concludes with [[Kelsa]] demanding that [[Orthos]] tell her the full story of [[Lindon]]'s survival and growth.}}
* '''Chapter 2:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Makiel]], The Hound{{!}}the Hound, uses a Celestial lenses{{!}}celestial lens to track down [[Iri]], [[the Angler of the Crystal Halls]], who has successfully stolen twelve experimental scythes from a secure [[Abidan]] [[vault]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]] undergoes rigorous and intimidating testing by [[Akura Justice]] to determine his eligibility for the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament. In a separate training session, [[Yerin]] pushes herself to her limits in a combat spar against [[Eithan]], eventually demanding that he teach her how to master his level of skill.}}
* '''Chapter 3:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After being introduced to the [[Akura clan]] as a protected guest and teammate for [[Mercy]], [[Lindon]] is attacked by her brother, [[Pride]], who challenges his worthiness. [[Lindon]] attempts to feign weakness to be sent home, but after a brief skirmish and an encounter with the [[Herald]] [[Fury]], he is settled into a private residence provided by the clan. While recovering in his basement and tending to his companions, [[Lindon]] is unexpectedly confronted by the [[Sage of the Silver Heart]], [[Charity]].}}
* '''Chapter 4:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After [[Akura Charity]] reveals that the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament is vital to the survival of the [[Blackflame Empire]] against [[dragon]] encroachment, [[Lindon]] commits to fighting and begins an intensive training regimen involving combat duels and [[Enforcer technique]] simulations. Meanwhile, [[Yerin]] and [[Naru Saeya]] receive a shipment of training supplies from the [[Akura clan]], prompting [[Eithan]] to suggest specialized sensory training for [[Yerin]]. The chapter concludes with the group learning that they have approximately eight months to prepare before being retrieved.}}
* '''Chapter 5:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Charity]] observes [[Lindon]]'s intense training through a magical owl and decides he needs more rest, but her father, the powerful [[Herald]] [[Akura Fury]], becomes intrigued by [[Lindon]]'s rapid progress. [[Fury]] visits [[Lindon]] in his basement, where he interacts with [[Lindon's Sylvan Riverseed]] and demonstrates several advanced ways to develop [[Lindon]]'s palm strike technique. After witnessing [[Lindon]]'s successful attempt at a complex variation, an excited [[Fury]] decides to stay and teach him even more possibilities.}}
* '''Chapter 6:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After being beaten by a girl with a hammer, [[Lindon]] accepts a challenge from an [[Akura]] [[Underlord]] using lightning, only to be defeated again. He is subsequently confronted and beaten by [[Akura Pride]], who challenges him to a future duel before the tournament. Later, [[Charity]] observes [[Lindon]]'s exhaustion and provides him with a transmission construct, which allows him to eagerly communicate with [[Yerin Arelius]].}}
* '''Chapter 7:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After a brief, bittersweet communication with [[Yerin]] via a jewel-[[construct]], [[Lindon]] prepares for a significant day in [[Moongrave]]. During an Akura clan{{!}}Akura family selection tournament, [[Lindon]] audaciously challenges all the remaining [[Underlord]] candidates to a duel. He successfully defeats multiple opponents, including [[Grace]], before [[Charity]] interrupts the fight against [[Pride]] to declare [[Lindon]]'s selection final.}}
* '''Chapter 8:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After attending a drake-dancing show with [[Mercy]], [[Lindon]] is presented to the [[Akura clan]] in a massive public ceremony before departing for the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament on a high-speed cloudship. Meanwhile, [[Yerin]], [[Eithan]], and [[Naru Saeya]] travel on a separate vessel when they are suddenly ambushed by the gold [[dragon]] Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara. The [[dragon]]'s attack severely damages their ship and depletes its fuel, leaving the group stranded and facing a month-long journey to reach the tournament on time.}}
* '''Chapter 9:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Upon arriving at the [[Ninecloud Court]] for the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament, [[Lindon]] and the [[Akura clan]] encounter the [[Winter Sage]], who reveals that her team was attacked by the [[dragon]] [[Herald]] [[Xorrus]]. The tension escalates when [[Xorrus]] arrives in a golden [[dragon]] form, mockingly dropping a piece of the destroyed [[Temple of Rising Earth]] onto the [[Akura]] ship before being restrained by the [[Court]]'s power. [[Lindon]] realizes that a powerful competitor with a personal grudge against him is favored to win, heightening the stakes for his own survival and the [[fate]] of the [[Blackflame Empire]].}}
* '''Chapter 10:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|As [[Yerin]], [[Eithan]], and [[Naru Saeya]] travel toward [[Ninecloud City]], they are rescued from their failing cloudship by the [[Winter Sage]], [[Min Shuei]]. Meanwhile, [[Lindon]], [[Mercy]], and [[Pride]] prepare for the [[Uncrowned King Tournament]] in the [[Ninecloud Court]], where [[Lindon]] is relieved to see [[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]] arrive just in time. The opening ceremony of the tournament begins with a grand introduction of all participating teams and [[Monarchs]], but is abruptly interrupted by the sudden appearance of [[Northstrider]].}}
* '''Chapter 11:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|The legendary [[Monarch]] [[Northstrider]] arrives at the tournament, seizing control of the competition and declaring himself the [[judge]]. During the first trial, [[Lindon]] uses his [[hunger madra]] to disrupt enemy constructs and lead a white army to victory while attempting to hide his true power. In the second trial, various competitors face a puzzle box, with [[Lindon]] using [[Dross]] to solve it quickly while others use force or social manipulation.}}
* '''Chapter 12:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] navigates a series of increasingly difficult trials, utilizing [[Dross]]'s assistance and his own unique abilities to climb the rankings to tenth place. After the first round concludes, he and [[Yerin]] review their performances and discuss potential improvements for the upcoming team elimination matches. The chapter ends with [[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] deciding to combine their individual prizes—customizable floating fortresses—into a single, larger residence.}}
* '''Chapter 13:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Eithan]] meticulously designs a flying fortress filled with valuable plants to maximize his prize from the [[Ninecloud Court]]. Meanwhile, competitors are transported to a tropical island for a round where they must secure and hold golden crowns to advance. As various teams engage in brutal combat with monsters and rival artists, [[Lindon]]'s team faces an ambush just as three crowns descend from the sky.}}
* '''Chapter 14:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] and his team attempt to ambush Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara and her [[dragon]] allies near a crown, successfully defeating several gold and red dragons and a Black dragons{{!}}black dragon. However, Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara uses Ghostwater{{!}}ghostwater to evade their attacks and kills [[Naru Saeya]], leaving [[Lindon]] severely injured. As Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara continues her assault, [[Lindon]] decides to pivot his strategy and grabs a crown from hiding.}}
* '''Chapter 15:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|During the second round of the tournament, Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara dominates the battlefield until [[Lindon]] uses a crown to neutralize her, though [[Eithan]] is seemingly dissolved by her retaliatory fire. Following the round, [[Lindon]] and [[Mercy]] learn they must face [[Naian Blackflame]], a broken prisoner of the Path of Black Flame{{!}}Blackflame Path, in a team-based match. [[Lindon]] proposes defeating [[Naian]] himself to send a powerful message of strength to the [[Monarchs]], who are currently divided over [[Reigan Shen]]'s dangerous plan to bait the [[Dreadgods]].}}
* '''Chapter 16:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] faces [[Naian Blackflame]] in an arena battle, using his [[hunger madra]] and [[Little Blue]]'s intervention to successfully stabilize [[Naian]]'s fractured spirit. Although the Akura prime team{{!}}Akura team wins the match, a gold [[dragon]] named Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara executes [[Naian]] in retaliation for his regained lucidity. The chapter concludes with [[Lindon]] mourning the fallen [[Blackflame]] and vowing to find a way to help [[Little Blue]], while [[Northstrider]] delivers [[Naian]]'s final words: "[[The dragon advances]]."}}
* '''Chapter 17:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After a tense encounter with the [[House Arelius]] team, [[Yerin]] and [[Eithan]] enter an arena designed with pillars to test their spiritual perception. [[Eithan]] wins a high-stakes duel against [[Veris Arelius]] by using an [[Archlord sacred dagger]] shaped like scissors to gain the upper hand. Seeking to master a heightened state of awareness she sensed during the fight, [[Yerin]] decides to enter her own match against [[Altavian Arelius]] while wearing a blindfold.}}
* '''Chapter 18:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Yerin]] wins her duel against Altavian Arelius{{!}}Altavian by fighting blindfolded, utilizing a heightened spiritual perception that mirrors the style of her late master. Following the match, [[Northstrider]] announces the final elimination round for the sixteen remaining competitors, setting up several high-stakes matchups including [[Yerin]] versus [[Mercy]] and [[Lindon]] versus [[Sopharanatoth]]. Meanwhile, a vision reveals the [[Vroshir]] [[Mad King]] meeting with the The Angler{{!}}Angler to exchange a [[Worldseed]] for a masterfully crafted scythe, an act that [[Makiel]] observes with growing dread.}}
* '''Chapter 19:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|As the Uncrowned King Tournament{{!}}Uncrowned King tournament enters its fourth round, various competitors receive powerful prizes from [[Monarchs]], including [[Lindon]], who receives an [[Iron Heart]] to enhance his healing. After intense training sessions to prepare for their upcoming matches, [[Lindon]] is shocked when the tournament brackets are unexpectedly altered by [[Monarch]] [[Northstrider]]. Instead of facing Sopharanatoth{{!}}Sophara, [[Lindon]] is forced into a single-elimination duel against his close ally and friend, [[Yerin]].}}
* '''Chapter 20:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]] and [[Yerin]] engage in an intense, high-stakes duel where [[Lindon]] initially struggles to fight back due to his reluctance to hurt her. After [[Dross]] helps him overcome his hesitation, [[Lindon]] commits to the fight, leading to a series of devastating technique exchanges involving [[Archlord madra]] and [[Blackflame]]. The chapter culminates in a massive clash between [[Yerin]]'s second [[Final Sword]] and [[Lindon]]'s original technique, The Dragon Descends{{!}}the Dragon Descends, which leaves the arena in ruins and captivates the watching [[Monarchs]] and [[Sages]].}}
* '''Chapter 21:''' {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|After losing his match to [[Yerin]], [[Lindon]] is comforted by [[Eithan]] before a heavenly messenger named [[Kiuran]] descends upon the arena to announce a new grand prize: a divine weapon called [[Penance]]. The messenger reveals that the [[Abidan]] are seeking to recruit more soldiers to combat [[the Mad King]], and he warns the [[Monarchs]] that the tournament's winner will be protected by heaven. As [[Lindon]] attempts to reach [[Yerin]] to warn her of the danger this prize poses, he is suddenly intercepted and confronted by [[Northstrider]].}}

== Characters ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Yerin]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Sopharanatoth]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Eithan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Dross]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Northstrider]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ninecloud Soul]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Charity]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Pride]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Reigan Shen]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Naru Saeya]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Sha Miara]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Altavian Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Frozen Blade]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ziel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Fury]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Grace]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Makiel]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Justice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Little Blue]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Charity]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Naian Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Orthos]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Yerin Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Mad King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Angler]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Weeping Dragon]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Naian]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Veris Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Mad King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Sword Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Wei Shi Lindon Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Malice]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Mercy]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Emriss Silentborn]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Dragon King]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Xorrus]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Sha Leiala]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Winter Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ekeri]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Min Shuei]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Veris]]}}

== Locations & groups ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura clan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Blackflame Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ninecloud Court]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Gold dragons]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[The Eight-Man Empire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Black dragons]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Rosegold continent]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Vroshir]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Stormcallers]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura prime team]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[House Arelius]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Abidan]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Arelius family]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Night Wheel Valley]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura Underlords]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Akura backup team]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ghost-Blades]]}}

== Abilities & sacred arts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Soul Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ruler technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Burning Cloak]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Striker techniques]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Striker technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Enforcer technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Path of Black Flame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[black dragon's breath]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Empty Palm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Blackflame madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Void Dragon’s Dance]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Soulfire]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Enforcer techniques]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Forged madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Forger technique]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Steelborn body]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|hunger binding}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Rippling Sword]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[shadow madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[force madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Full-body Enforcer technique]]}}

== Artifacts & constructs ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Wavedancer]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon's shield]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Emperor's crown (Cradle)]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Thousand-Mile Cloud]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Lindon's skeletal Remnant arm]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|cloudship}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|transmission construct}}

== Events & concepts ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Uncrowned King Tournament]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[pure madra]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Blackflame]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Underlord]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Uncrowned]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Dreadgods]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Blood Shadow]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Monarch]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Sage]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Path of the Hungry Deep]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[Ghostwater]]}} • {{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|[[dragons]]}}

== Sources ==
* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cradle
* https://openlibrary.org/works/OL20857119W

{{Willverse books nav}}

[[Category:Books]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
